title: The strict-gate era closes: all four blocking constraints content-matched, a87 expressed as conformance predicates
context: Rounds 1-3 shipped store-layer changes through a documented script workaround because con_902759b3dc and con_a87360128b were scope-only: every Edit under src/store/** denied at strict firmness regardless of content. a87 ('vectors derived, never source of truth') resists a text matcher by nature — the violation is a data-flow direction, not a line pattern.
decision: Two-layer close. EDIT-time: con_902759b3dc forbids added raw writes (writeFileSync/writeFile patterns — the actual atomicity bypass); con_a87360128b forbids re-adding better-sqlite3 (the retired dep) or new direct DatabaseSync usage (the drift toward sqlite-as-truth); both stores updated (overlay copy wins recs dedupe). COMMIT/CONFORM-time: a87's real invariant lives as conformance predicates on its source decision dec_08a685fca2 — loadAll not-calls openDb, loadAll not-calls DatabaseSync (transitive: the JSON read path can never touch sqlite), pruneStaleEmbeddings calls embedHash (reconciliation is content-hash-driven) — all green. Proof of era-close: the first clean Edit-tool write to hunchStore.ts since the gates existed passed with grounding injected, no deny, no script.
alternatives_rejected: A text matcher faking a87's data-flow invariant (patterns like 'sqlite.*source of truth'): never-firing rules are worse than blunt ones — the predicate layer is where flow invariants belong
Narrowing a87's scope to db.ts/schema.ts/embedder.ts: hunchStore's reindex/reconcile paths are exactly where the invariant matters
Lowering firmness: precision, not permissiveness — same call as round 2's matchers
